description: Emotions: how the main character feels at the start of the story. Pre-emotions go before the character name, post-emotions go after.
preEmotions: Afraid of what might happen next.
Driven by a need to know and explore.
Aching for a place far away.
Wishing they could disappear from everyone's gaze.
Standing tall with satisfaction in what they've done.
Anxious about something that hasn't happened yet.
Willing to face danger despite uncertainty.
Lost in a tangle of conflicting thoughts.
Set on a goal with unwavering focus.
Reluctant to be noticed or speak up.
Unable to sit still, needing change or action.
Deeply thankful for what they have.
Crushed under the weight of too much at once.
Refusing to bend or change course.
Carrying the weight of something they did wrong.
Struggling to wait for what they want.
Carrying the pain of a deep loss.
Watchful and distrustful of those around them.
Looking for trouble with a gleam in their eye.
Worn out in body and spirit.
Unafraid in the face of danger.
Pushing back against rules and expectations.
Calm and content with the world as it is.
Holding onto resentment that won't fade.
Feeling invisible, as if no one remembers them.
At the end of their rope, willing to try anything.
Stunned by something amazing beyond words.
postEmotions: Burning to show what they can do.
Excluded from something everyone else is part of.
Terrified of what hides in the shadows.
Carrying an empty space where a person used to be.
Keeping something locked inside that wants to come out.
Wearing a mask of normalcy over hidden pain.
Holding back tears through sheer willpower.
Fleeing from a problem they can't face.
Surrounded by faces but uncertain of any.
Displaced and longing for familiar ground.
Facing something bigger than they can handle alone.
Putting on courage like a coat that doesn't quite fit.
At the very edge of quitting.
Unable to shake a moment from the past.
Imagining a life beyond their current world.
